question_text: A light wave has a frequency of 5 × 10¹⁴ Hz and travels through a vacuum at 3 × 10⁸ m/s. Calculate the wavelength of this light and identify which colour of the visible spectrum it corresponds to.
correct_answer: 6 × 10⁻⁷ m or 600 nm; this corresponds to orange light
explanation: Using the wave equation v = fλ, we rearrange to find λ = v/f = (3 × 10⁸)/(5 × 10¹⁴) = 6 × 10⁻⁷ m. This wavelength of 600 nanometres falls in the orange region of the visible spectrum (between red at ~700 nm and yellow at ~580 nm).
